creator: freebsd@mldelaney.com
text:
On FreeBSD 13.0-RELEASE-p10, after installing gitlab-ce-14.8.4 from pkg+http://pkg.FreeBSD.org/${ABI}/latest. When I attempt to start gitlab-ce after installation, I get the following:




Bundler could not find compatible versions for gem "chef-utils":
  In Gemfile:
    ohai (~> 16.10) was resolved to 16.17.0, which depends on
      chef-config (>= 14.12, < 17) was resolved to 16.17.18, which depends on
        chef-utils (= 16.17.18)

    ohai (~> 16.10) was resolved to 16.17.0, which depends on
      chef-utils (>= 16.0, < 17)

Could not find gem 'chef-utils (= 16.17.18)', which is required by gem 'ohai (~> 16.10)', in any of the sources.

Bundler could not find compatible versions for gem "fog-core":
  In Gemfile:
    fog-core (= 2.1.0)

    fog-aliyun (~> 0.3) was resolved to 0.3.19, which depends on
      fog-core

    fog-google (~> 1.15) was resolved to 1.18.0, which depends on
      fog-core (< 2.3)

    fog-local (~> 0.6) was resolved to 0.8.0, which depends on
      fog-core (>= 1.27, < 3.0)

    fog-openstack (~> 1.0) was resolved to 1.0.11, which depends on
      fog-core (~> 2.1)

    fog-rackspace (~> 0.1.1) was resolved to 0.1.6, which depends on
      fog-core (>= 1.35)

    gitlab-fog-azure-rm (~> 1.2.0) was resolved to 1.2.0, which depends on
      fog-core (= 2.1.0)

Bundler could not find compatible versions for gem "fugit":
  In Gemfile:
    fugit (~> 1.2.1)

    sidekiq-cron (~> 1.2) was resolved to 1.2.0, which depends on
      fugit (~> 1.1)

Bundler could not find compatible versions for gem "omniauth":
  In Gemfile:
    omniauth (~> 1.8)

    gitlab-omniauth-openid-connect (~> 0.9.0) was resolved to 0.9.1, which depends on
      omniauth (~> 1.9)

    gitlab_omniauth-ldap (~> 2.1.1) was resolved to 2.1.1, which depends on
      omniauth (~> 1.3)

    omniauth-atlassian-oauth2 (~> 0.2.0) was resolved to 0.2.0, which depends on
      omniauth (>= 1.1.1)

    omniauth-cas3 (~> 1.1.4) was resolved to 1.1.4, which depends on
      omniauth (~> 1.2)

    omniauth-github (~> 1.4) was resolved to 1.4.0, which depends on
      omniauth (~> 1.5)

    omniauth-gitlab (~> 1.0.2) was resolved to 1.0.4, which depends on
      omniauth (~> 1.0)

    omniauth-twitter (~> 1.4) was resolved to 1.4.0, which depends on
      omniauth-oauth (~> 1.1) was resolved to 1.2.0, which depends on
        omniauth (>= 1.0, < 3)

    omniauth-saml (~> 1.10) was resolved to 1.10.3, which depends on
      omniauth (~> 1.3, >= 1.3.2)

    omniauth-shibboleth (~> 1.3.0) was resolved to 1.3.0, which depends on
      omniauth (>= 1.0.0)
Bundler could not find compatible versions for gem "chef-utils":
  In Gemfile:
    ohai (~> 16.10) was resolved to 16.17.0, which depends on
      chef-config (>= 14.12, < 17) was resolved to 16.17.18, which depends on
        chef-utils (= 16.17.18)

    ohai (~> 16.10) was resolved to 16.17.0, which depends on
      chef-utils (>= 16.0, < 17)

Could not find gem 'chef-utils (= 16.17.18)', which is required by gem 'ohai (~> 16.10)', in any of the sources.

  In Gemfile:
    fog-core (= 2.1.0)

    fog-aliyun (~> 0.3) was resolved to 0.3.19, which depends on

    fog-openstack (~> 1.0) was resolved to 1.0.11, which depends on
      fog-core (~> 2.1)

    fog-rackspace (~> 0.1.1) was resolved to 0.1.6, which depends on
      fog-core (>= 1.35)

    gitlab-fog-azure-rm (~> 1.2.0) was resolved to 1.2.0, which depends on

    omniauth-twitter (~> 1.4) was resolved to 1.4.0, which depends on

      omniauth (~> 1.3, >= 1.3.2)

    omniauth-shibboleth (~> 1.3.0) was resolved to 1.3.0, which depends on
      omniauth (>= 1.0.0)
Don't run Bundler as root. Bundler can ask for sudo if it is needed, and installing your bundle as root will break this application for all non-root users on this machine.
